post.summary: Analyze a document and return transaction suggestions
Confirm and create transactions from reviewed suggestions
post.description: Each accepted item should reference existing wallet/party/category rows by ID. When an ID is omitted and the matching auto_create_* flag is true, the analyzer's suggested name is used to create the resource (wallets additionally need a currency).

index.description: Zero-based position of the suggestion in the session.

wallet_id.description: Existing wallet owned by the caller. Required unless auto_create_wallets is true and the suggestion has a wallet name and currency.

party_id.description: Existing party owned by the caller.

category_id.description: Existing category owned by the caller.

auto_create_wallets.description: Create a wallet from the suggestion when wallet_id is omitted.

auto_create_parties.description: Create a party from the suggestion when party_id is omitted.

auto_create_categories.description: Create a category from the suggestion when category_id is omitted.

post.summary: Mark a notification as read or sync its client_id
post.description: Marks the notification as read using the supplied read_at (or now() if omitted). If only client_id is sent, the server records the client_id without changing read state, so the mobile sync can establish the link before the user opens the notification.

client_id.description: Client-generated identifier for offline sync. Optional.

read_at.description: Timestamp the notification was read on the client. Optional. When omitted and client_id is also omitted, the server uses the current time. When omitted and client_id is present, read state is left unchanged.

200.description: Notification marked as read or synced

post.summary: Mark an income transaction as a refund
post.description:
Mark an income transaction as a refund, optionally linking it to
the original expense it refunded. Only income transactions can be
refunds; the original (if provided) must be an expense owned by
the same user.

client_id.description: Client-generated ID for the new refund row

original_transaction_id.description: Server id of the expense being refunded

original_client_id.description: Client id of the expense being refunded (used when original has not synced yet)

422.description: Only income transactions can be refunds, or invalid original

delete.summary: Remove the refund flag from a transaction

id.description:
A Refund row marks an income transaction as refunding money received
back from a prior expense. The `original_transaction_id` link is
optional — users sometimes log a refund without remembering or caring
which specific expense it came from. See the `Refundable` trait on
Transaction for the query helpers.

original_transaction_id.description: Optional link to the expense being refunded
